_comment: Copy this file to deploy/params.json and fill in values. params.json is gitignored. Do NOT put secrets in this file — secrets (AAD client secret) are prompted at runtime or supplied via the SPOCS_AAD_CLIENT_SECRET environment variable, then stored in Key Vault.
_naming_comment: All names must follow Azure resource naming rules. storageAccount/keyVault/sqlServer/search/serviceBus/webApp are GLOBALLY unique — pick something likely to be free.

sku._comment: appServicePlan must support AlwaysOn for WebJobs. B2 or above recommended for production.

azureAd._comment: Used by both scripts. If you run deploy-spo.ps1 AadApp phase first, it will create the AAD app and write clientId + servicePrincipalObjectId back here.
azureAd._keyVaultObjectId_comment: Object ID of the service principal for the above app registration. Used to grant it Key Vault Secrets+Certificates User so the workers can pull the cert at runtime.

aadApp._comment: displayName MUST match the 'resource' string in src/SPFx/spfx-cold-storage/config/package-solution.json (currently 'SPO Cold Storage Web API') so the SPFx webApiPermissionRequest grants succeed.

certificate._comment: Used by deploy-spo.ps1 -Phase Cert. source = 'generate' | 'file' | 'keyvault'. 'generate' creates a self-signed cert in deploy/.local/ (gitignored) and uploads to KV. 'file' uses your existing PFX (set pfxPath, supply password via -PfxPassword or SPOCS_PFX_PASSWORD env var). 'keyvault' assumes the cert already exists in KV under azureAd.certificateName.
certificate.subject: CN=AzureAutomationSPOAccess

sql._comment: Entra-only auth. Provide an Entra group (recommended) or user that will be SQL admin. The deploying user MUST be a member, because the script grants the Web App MSI db_owner via T-SQL post-deploy.

sharePoint._comment: baseServerAddress is the SharePoint Online root (used as the BaseServerAddress app setting). appCatalogUrl + targetSiteRelativeUrl are only needed for deploy-spo.ps1 -Phase SpfxDeploy.

storage._comment: userDataReaders: object IDs of end-users (or a group) that should get 'Storage Blob Data Reader' on the storage account. The SPA calls Blob Storage directly with the signed-in user's token, so without this they'll see 'AuthorizationPermissionMismatch'. Each entry can be a bare GUID (defaults to type=User) or {objectId, type} where type is User|Group|ServicePrincipal. Recommended pattern: create one Entra group containing all SPA users, put its objectId here.

network._comment: Set to false to leave the deploy IP in the SQL firewall after Sql phase. Useful for debugging.
